title: Add podman pod prune
body:
podman system prune would leave pods be, and not prune them if they were stopped.
Fix this by adding a `podman pod prune` command that prunes stopped pods similarly to containers.

Signed-off-by: Peter Hunt <pehunt@redhat.com>

comments.body: Note: I am debating adding a pod state called `OnlyInfra` or something indicating the only running container in the pod is the infra container. If every other container in the pod is stopped then it may be worth also pruning those. WDYT
code LGTM, could you fix the man pages?
do i read the code correctly that you wired it for the remote client but didn't actually implement it?
@baude yeah that's correct. It seems containers prune is also not implemented, so I figured pods prune not being implemented would be okay

> @baude yeah that's correct. It seems containers prune is also not implemented, so I figured pods prune not being implemented would be okay

We just havent gotten to containers prune.  So please do the pods prune. Much appreciated.  I'm available for help and questions.
5 hours later.... remote pod prune exists :) ptal @baude @jwhonce
